Los Ántrax.description: Los Ántrax is a large enforcer unit and hit squad for the Sinaloa Cartel, based in Culiacán, Sinaloa, Mexico. The group provides armed security to drug lord Ismael 'El Mayo' Zambada and is responsible for numerous homicides and violent attacks. It is considered the Sinaloa Cartel's largest and deadliest enforcer unit.

descriptions: Los Antrax is listed as an ally of Gente Nueva in the infobox.
edges.evidence_quote: Allies: Sinaloa Cartel , Artistas Asesinos , Los Mexicles , Los Antrax

descriptions: Los Ántrax is listed as a rival of La Línea in the infobox.
edges.evidence_quote: Rivals: Sinaloa Cartel , Los Ántrax , Gente Nueva , Los Mexicles , Artistas Asesinos

descriptions: Los Ántrax are rivals of Los Mazatlecos.
edges.evidence_quote: Rivals: ... Los Ántrax

descriptions: Los Ántrax and Beltrán Leyva Cartel are rivals.
edges.evidence_quote: Rivals: Los Mazatlecos, Los Zetas, Mexican Army, Cártel de los Beltrán-Leyva

descriptions: Los Ántrax and Gente Nueva are allies.
edges.evidence_quote: Allies: Sinaloa Cartel, Gente Nueva, Los Chapitos

descriptions: Los Ántrax and Los Chapitos are allies.
edges.evidence_quote: Allies: Sinaloa Cartel, Gente Nueva, Los Chapitos

descriptions: Los Ántrax and Los Mazatlecos are rivals.
edges.evidence_quote: Rivals: Los Mazatlecos, Los Zetas, Mexican Army, Cártel de los Beltrán-Leyva

descriptions: Los Ántrax and Los Zetas are rivals.
edges.evidence_quote: Rivals: Los Mazatlecos, Los Zetas, Mexican Army, Cártel de los Beltrán-Leyva

edges.evidence_quote: Los Ántrax is a large enforcer unit and hit squad for the Sinaloa Cartel

descriptions: Los Ántrax is an enforcer death squad for the Sinaloa Cartel.
edges.evidence_quote: The Sinaloa Cartel has been known for having enforcer death squads like ... Los Ántrax

descriptions: Los Ántrax is an armed squadron formed to protect a founding member of the Sinaloa Cartel.
edges.evidence_quote: He was a leader and founding member of Los Ántrax, an armed squadron formed to protect Ismael 'El Mayo' Zambada García, founding member of the Sinaloa Cartel.

descriptions: Los Ántrax is an enforcer unit of Sinaloa Cartel.
edges.evidence_quote: the cartel's sub-groups and allies are known to consist of Los Ántrax (enforcer unit)

descriptions: Los Ántrax is the armed wing of Sinaloa.
edges.evidence_quote: fundadores del brazo armado del CDS "Los Ántrax"

descriptions: Los Ántrax was an armed squad protecting a Sinaloa leader.
edges.evidence_quote: Los Ántrax, un escuadrón armado que protegía a Ismael «el Mayo» Zambada García, líder máximo del cártel.

descriptions: Los Charritos is a hitsquad that splintered from the Beltrán-Leyva Organization.
edges.evidence_quote: Los Charritos Most active hitsquad believed to have a heavy presence in the US (Chicago, Atlanta, Los Angeles, Las Vegas)

edges.evidence_quote: Giuseppe Calò from Porta Nuova

descriptions: The two clans were in a feud.

descriptions: RAAD merged with Real IRA to form New IRA.
edges.evidence_quote: It is a continuation of the Real Irish Republican Army (Real IRA), which began to be called the 'New IRA' in July 2012 when Republican Action Against Drugs (RAAD) and other small republican militant groups merged with it.

descriptions: RAAD merged with the Real IRA to form the New IRA.
edges.evidence_quote: RAAD, 'the IRA', and some smaller groups merged with the Real IRA in 2012 to form the New IRA.

descriptions: The Real IRA merged with RAAD in July 2012.
edges.evidence_quote: In July 2012, it was reported that Republican Action Against Drugs (RAAD) and other small republican militant groups were merging with the Real IRA.

descriptions: RAAD merged with the Real IRA in 2012.
edges.evidence_quote: On 26 July 2012, it was reported that Republican Action Against Drugs (RAAD) and other small republican militant groups were merging with the Real IRA.

descriptions: RAAD merged with the Real IRA to form the New IRA.
edges.evidence_quote: RAAD, 'the IRA', and some smaller groups merged with the Real IRA in 2012 to form the New IRA.

descriptions: Thomas and De Vita were in conflict in the San Ferdinando feud.
edges.evidence_quote: Faida di San Ferdinando, tra i Thomas e i De Vita.

descriptions: NPK and Shankstarz engaged in a fight in 2007.
edges.evidence_quote: In 2007, a fight occurred between members of the NPK and the Shankstarz, a gang from neighbouring Edmonton, following an attempted ambush on two Shankstarz members; the ambush was revenge for the attack of an NPK member at a club in Forest Gate.

descriptions: Brass Knuckles MC is a Bandidos support club.
edges.evidence_quote: charges against Decarlo, Heredia and Robert Farrell Grant III, sergeant-at-arms of the Bandidos support club Brass Knuckles MC, were dismissed

descriptions: Dubois Brothers and McSween gang fought a turf war starting in 1973.
edges.evidence_quote: The war between the two groups, Dubois Brothers and the McSween gang, broke out in 1973.

descriptions: McSween gang became associates of Dubois Brothers in 1957, cooperating in criminal activities.
edges.evidence_quote: The Dubois Brothers had the McSweens, an Irish-Canadian gang, join their associates in 1957 after one of the Brothers met Pierre McSween in reform school while serving a two-year term for theft.

descriptions: The Bontade family was a primary rival of the Corleonesi.
edges.evidence_quote: The Corleonesi's primary rivals were Stefano Bontade , Salvatore Inzerillo and Tano Badalamenti , bosses of various powerful Palermo Mafia families.

descriptions: Motisi clan had rivalry with Stefano Bontade and his clan.
edges.evidence_quote: Rivals: Inzerillo Mafia clan , Stefano Bontade

Cártel Independiente de Sonora.description: Mexican criminal federation dedicated to drug trafficking, extortion, money laundering, and other illegal activities. Originally formed as Gente Nueva, the armed wing of the Sinaloa Cartel, it became independent in 2023 after internal disputes with Los Chapitos. Based in Sonora, it operates in Chihuahua, Sonora, and Sinaloa, and is currently active.

descriptions: Los Salazar is listed as an ally of Artistas Asesinos in the infobox.
edges.evidence_quote: Aliados: Cártel de Sinaloa , Gente Nueva , * Los Salazar , *

descriptions: Cártel Independiente de Sonora was formerly known as Gente Nueva.
edges.evidence_quote: El Cártel Independiente de Sonora , también conocidos como Los Paredes y Los Salazar, es una federación criminal mexicana dedicada al narcotráfico , extorsión , lavado de dinero , entre otras actividades delictivas. Anteriormente Gente Nueva

descriptions: Cártel Independiente de Sonora was created to counter and destroy the influence of Juárez Cartel.
edges.evidence_quote: creado para contrarrestar, combatir y destruir la influencia del Cártel de Juárez en el noroeste de México

descriptions: Cártel Independiente de Sonora was created to counter La Línea, the execution arm of Juárez Cartel.
edges.evidence_quote: así como para contrarrestar el accionar de La Línea , que actualmente es la célula más grande dentro del Cártel de Juárez.

descriptions: Cártel Independiente de Sonora split from Los Chapitos faction in 2023 due to internal disputes.
edges.evidence_quote: Para el año 2023, se escindieron y pasaron a ser independientes, debido a disputas internas con la facción de Los Chapitos, también conocidos como La Chapiza del Cártel de Sinaloa.

descriptions: Los Salazar is the armed wing of the Sinaloa Cartel.
edges.evidence_quote: the Los Salazar criminal cell, identified as the armed wing of the Sinaloa Cartel

descriptions: Los Salazar is a cell of the Sinaloa Cartel.
edges.evidence_quote: Los Salazar, a powerful cell of the Sinaloa Cartel, had by this point managed to build a significant presence in Ciudad Juárez as well.

descriptions: Los Salazar is a powerful cell of the Sinaloa Cartel.
edges.evidence_quote: Los Salazar, una poderosa célula del cártel de Sinaloa

descriptions: Cártel Independiente de Sonora was formerly the elite armed wing of Sinaloa Cartel.
edges.evidence_quote: Anteriormente Gente Nueva , fue uno de los brazos armados mejor entrenados y experimentados que funcionaban como Fuerzas Especiales del Cártel de Sinaloa (FECDS) y era una de las ramas de élite del Cártel de Sinaloa

descriptions: El Cártel Independiente de Sonora es enemigo de Los Chapitos según el infobox.
edges.evidence_quote: Enemigos: Cártel Independiente de Sonora

descriptions: Los Salazar is a cell of the Sinaloa Cartel.
edges.evidence_quote: Los Salazar, a powerful cell of the Sinaloa Cartel, had by this point also managed to build a significant presence in Ciudad Juárez

descriptions: Los Salazar is the armed wing of Sinaloa.
edges.evidence_quote: identificada como brazo armado del Cártel de Sinaloa

descriptions: The River Gang shared bootlegging rackets and underworld activities with the East Side Gang, which became the Detroit Partnership.
edges.evidence_quote: Milazzo's Mafia group and the Eastside Gang maintained alliances with other mob factions, including the River Gang, led by brothers Thomas and Peter Licavoli who shared the local bootlegging rackets and various underworld activities with each other.

descriptions: The River Gang was a predecessor group that later evolved into the Detroit Partnership.
edges.evidence_quote: During the Prohibition era, a gang of Detroit mafiosi known as the River Gang smuggled Canadian beer and liquor into the United States from Ontario and controlled most of the bootlegging market in Southeast Michigan.

Los Pepes.description: Colombian paramilitary group formed in 1992 to fight Pablo Escobar and the Medellín Cartel. Financed by the Cali Cartel and led by the Castaño brothers, they waged a small-scale war involving assassinations and bombings. The group disbanded after Escobar's death in 1993, with many members later joining the United Self-Defense Forces of Colombia (AUC).

descriptions: Cali Cartel helped form Los Pepes to target Pablo Escobar.
edges.evidence_quote: helped form the vigilante group "Los Pepes"

descriptions: Cali Cartel financed Los Pepes.
edges.evidence_quote: The group was largely financed by the Cali Cartel and led by brothers Carlos and Fidel Castaño whom were right-wing paramilitary leading commanders that were actually once part of the Medellín Cartel.

descriptions: Cali Cartel funded Los Pepes to target Medellín Cartel.
edges.evidence_quote: The Calí Cartel provided $50 million to pay for weapons, informants, and assassins, with the hopes that they could wipe out their primary rival in the cocaine business.

descriptions: Cartel de Cali funded Los Pepes to fight Pablo Escobar.
edges.evidence_quote: el Cartel de Cali abrió su ataque inyectando de efectivo los bolsillos de « Los Pepes », organización criminal conformada por exmiembros del Cartel de Medellín y paramilitares, constituida para acabar con Escobar.

descriptions: Los Pepes were financed by the Cartel de Cali.
edges.evidence_quote: el grupo de Los Pepes , financiado por el Cartel de Cali y los paramilitares

descriptions: El Cartel de Cali financió a Los Pepes.
edges.evidence_quote: Los Pepes ... fue un grupo narcoparamilitar colombiano conformado por narcotraficantes ex-socios de Pablo Escobar y financiado principalmente por la cúpula del Cartel de Cali

descriptions: Cali Cartel leader Herrera was a founding member of Los Pepes.
edges.evidence_quote: Herrera is believed to have been a founding member of Los Pepes

descriptions: Cali Cartel founded Los Pepes to target Medellín Cartel.
edges.evidence_quote: the Cali Cartel grew in strength, eventually founding Los Pepes, or Perseguidos por Pablo Escobar ("People Persecuted by Pablo Escobar"). Los Pepes was specifically formed to target the Medellín Cartel and bring about the downfall of Pablo Escobar.

descriptions: Los Pepes are listed as rivals of Medellín Cartel in the infobox.
edges.evidence_quote: Rivals: Cali Cartel (defunct) , Los Pepes (defunct)

descriptions: Los Pepes targeted and killed Medellín Cartel lieutenants.
edges.evidence_quote: In 1993, shortly before Escobar's death, the cartel lieutenants were also targeted by the vigilante group Los Pepes (or PEPES, People Persecuted by Pablo Escobar).

descriptions: Los Pepes were enemies of the Medellín Cartel from 1992 to 1993.
edges.evidence_quote: Enemigos: ... Los Pepes (1992-1993).

descriptions: Los Pepes were formed to kill associates and family members of Pablo Escobar, targeting the Cartel de Medellín.
edges.evidence_quote: hizo su aparición pública la estructura que se autodenominó “Los Pepes” (Perseguidos por Pablo Escobar), detrás de la que estaban los Castaño y que se dedicaría a matar a los testaferros, escoltas, contadores, abogados y familiares del capo

descriptions: Dissidents from Medellín Cartel helped form Los Pepes to target Pablo Escobar.
edges.evidence_quote: dissidents within the Medellín Cartel, and the MAS worked together to create a new paramilitary organization known as Perseguidos Por Pablo Escobar

descriptions: Los Pepes emerged as a splinter faction from Los Choneros.
edges.evidence_quote: In Manabí, government crackdowns on the Choneros led to the emergence of violent splinter factions, including Los Pepes, a coalition of eight smaller gangs.

descriptions: Miembros de Los Pepes se convirtieron en líderes de las AUC.
edges.evidence_quote: Otros se consolidarían como líderes de las autodefensas, que se agruparían años después bajo el nombre de AUC

descriptions: Former Los Pepes leaders became leaders of AUC.
edges.evidence_quote: several of their leaders eventually went on to become leaders of a national paramilitary alliance in Colombia, the United Self-Defense Forces of Colombia (AUC)

descriptions: Cali Cartel provided financial support to Los Pepes.
edges.evidence_quote: The group was financed by the Cali Cartel

descriptions: Los Pepes allied with Cartel de Cali against Pablo Escobar.
edges.evidence_quote: en 1992 los Castaño habían formado parte de Los Pepes (Perseguidos por Pablo Escobar), en alianza con el Cartel de Cali , enemigos de Pablo Escobar

descriptions: El Cartel de Cali financió a Los Pepes.
edges.evidence_quote: Los Pepes ... financiado principalmente por la cúpula del Cartel de Cali

descriptions: Los Pepes was created to fight the Medellín Cartel.
edges.evidence_quote: Los Pepes was specifically formed to target the Medellín Cartel and bring about the downfall of Pablo Escobar.

descriptions: Los Pepes fought a war against the Medellín Cartel.
edges.evidence_quote: They waged a small-scale war against the Medellín Cartel in 1993

descriptions: Los Pepes was formed to fight the Medellín Cartel.
edges.evidence_quote: both the Norte del Valle Cartel and Cali Cartel formed and operated a vigilante group called Los Pepes ... to bring about the downfall of Pablo Escobar

descriptions: Los Pepes atacaron al Cartel de Medellín.
edges.evidence_quote: El grupo atacó al cartel de Medellín y más directamente a amigos, familiares y socios de Pablo Escobar

descriptions: Los Pepes se asociaron con el Cartel del Norte del Valle.
edges.evidence_quote: no dudaron en asociarse con los enemigos de Escobar, particularmente el cartel de Cali y el cartel del Norte del Valle

descriptions: A former Los Pepes member became a leader in Oficina de Envigado.
edges.evidence_quote: Another member of Los Pepes, Diego "Don Berna" Murillo Bejarano eventually became Inspector General of the AUC, as well as an important drug trafficker with Oficina de Envigado.

descriptions: Don Berna, miembro de Los Pepes, lideró la Oficina de Envigado.
edges.evidence_quote: Diego Murillo Bejarano alias «Don Berna», se convirtió con el tiempo en líder de las AUC, así como en un importante narcotraficante de la Oficina de Envigado

descriptions: The Puerto Rican Mob had small connections with Los Pepes.
edges.evidence_quote: small connections with Los Pepes

descriptions: The Estrella Family had small connections with Los Pepes.
edges.evidence_quote: small connections with Los Pepes

descriptions: MAS collaborated with Cali Cartel and Medellín Cartel dissidents to form Los Pepes.
edges.evidence_quote: the Calí Cartel, dissidents within the Medellín Cartel, and the MAS worked together to create a new paramilitary organization known as Perseguidos Por Pablo Escobar

descriptions: Oficina de Envigado allied with Los Pepes against Escobar.
edges.evidence_quote: Murillo fell out with Escobar and eventually joined forces with Los Pepes in order to oust him.

Motisi Mafia clan.description: Historical Sicilian Mafia clan from the Pagliarelli area in Palermo, founded in 1936. One of the oldest active Mafia clans in Palermo, it has been involved in the Sicilian Mafia Commission and allied with the Corleonesi. Members have been convicted for murders of anti-mafia judges and rival clan members. Currently led by Giovanni Motisi, a fugitive since 1993.

descriptions: Motisi clan had rivalry with Stefano Bontade and his clan.
edges.evidence_quote: Rivals: Inzerillo Mafia clan , Stefano Bontade

descriptions: Motisi clan was allied with the Corleonesi.
edges.evidence_quote: Allies: Corleonesi (defunct)

descriptions: Motisi clan had rivalry with Inzerillo clan.
edges.evidence_quote: Rivals: Inzerillo Mafia clan , Stefano Bontade

descriptions: Avenues 13 is a main rival of Westside Locos 13.
edges.evidence_quote: The gang's main rivals include the nearby Toonerville Rifa 13 , Elmwood st 13, Avenues 13

descriptions: Gooch gang allied with Old Trafford Cripz.
edges.evidence_quote: Gooch formed alliances with splinter groups including the Pit Bull Crew, Rusholme Crips, Fallowfield Mandem/Mad Dogs, Old Trafford Cripz, and Longsight Street Soldiers

descriptions: Infoboox lists Old Trafford Crips as ally.
edges.evidence_quote: Allies: Old Trafford Crips

descriptions: Old Trafford Cripz are affiliated with Gooch.
edges.evidence_quote: Old Trafford Cripz, a younger gang from Old Trafford affiliated with Gooch

descriptions: Old Trafford Cripz cooperated with Gooch factions in the murder of Giuseppe Gregory.
edges.evidence_quote: This murder was carried out by younger Gooch factions; Moses "Mojo" Mathias of the Young Gooch Close Gang, Njabulo Ndlovu of the Old Trafford Cripz and Hiruy Zerihun of the Fallowfield Mad Dogs

descriptions: Old Trafford Cripz were allied with Gooch Close Gang.
edges.evidence_quote: the OTC were the most active soldiers on the Gooch side

descriptions: Old Trafford Cripz were in conflict with Haydock Close Crew.

descriptions: Old Trafford Cripz were in conflict with Longsight Crew.
edges.evidence_quote: as well as the LSC

descriptions: Old Trafford Cripz were in conflict with Moss Side Bloods.
edges.evidence_quote: Trading shots with the younger Doddington clique known as the Moss Side Bloods

Autodefensas Unidas de Colombia.description: Colombian far-right paramilitary, drug trafficking, and counterinsurgent organization active from 1997 to 2006. Formed on April 18, 1997, as a coalition of paramilitary groups to combat leftist guerrillas such as FARC-EP and ELN. Responsible for numerous human rights abuses, including massacres, forced displacement, and assassinations. Designated as a terrorist organization by Colombia, the EU, and the US until 2014. Demobilized in 2006, with some members later forming successor criminal groups.

descriptions: 'Ndrangheta clans were closely associated with AUC paramilitary groups.
edges.evidence_quote: The 'Ndrangheta clans were closely associated with the AUC paramilitary groups led by Salvatore Mancuso

descriptions: 'Ndrangheta dealt directly with the AUC in cocaine trafficking.
edges.evidence_quote: Quando si inserisce nel traffico di cocaina negli anni ottanta e comincia a controllarne i traffici si sposta in Spagna , Portogallo , Paesi Bassi fino ad arrivare in Colombia e a trattare direttamente con i Narcos e con l' AUC di Salvatore Mancuso Gómez .

descriptions: The Mancuso have contacts with the AUC.
edges.evidence_quote: Hanno contatti con ... le Autodefensas Unidas de Colombia (AUC)

descriptions: Mancuso has contacts with AUC.
edges.evidence_quote: Hanno contatti con ... le Autodefensas Unidas de Colombia (AUC)

descriptions: Mancuso 'ndrina historically had contacts with AUC.
edges.evidence_quote: historically with the ... United Self-Defense Forces of Colombia (AUC)

descriptions: ACC refused to join AUC, considering it a drug trafficking project.
edges.evidence_quote: Las ACC no entraron a las Autodefensas Unidas de Colombia, una especie de grupo federado que buscó avalar a todos los grupos paramilitares del país creada por los hermanos Castaño en 1997, pues consideraban que era un proyecto del narcotráfico.

descriptions: AUC cooperated with 'Ndrangheta in cocaine trafficking.
edges.evidence_quote: Under the leadership of Salvatore Mancuso, son of Italian immigrants, the AUC maintained close links with the Calabrian 'Ndrangheta concerning cocaine trafficking.

descriptions: ACC is listed as an enemy of AUC in the infobox.
edges.evidence_quote: Enemigos: FARC-EP , ELN , Disidencias del EPL , ACC , Bloque Metro (Disidencia de AUC)

descriptions: The AUC's Bloque Centauros attacked the ACC.
edges.evidence_quote: El Bloque Centauros emprendería una serie de ataques en contra de un grupo paramilitar que militaba la zona, que era conocido por el nombre de Autodefensas Campesinas de Casanare (ACC).

descriptions: ACMM merged to form AUC.
edges.evidence_quote: Al conformarse las AUC el 18 de abril de 1997, las Autodefensas Campesinas de Córdoba y Urabá, las del Magdalena Medio y las de los Llanos Orientales se unieron creando las Autodefensas Unidas de Colombia (AUC)

descriptions: Autodefensas de los Llanos Orientales merged to form AUC.
edges.evidence_quote: Al conformarse las AUC el 18 de abril de 1997, las Autodefensas Campesinas de Córdoba y Urabá, las del Magdalena Medio y las de los Llanos Orientales se unieron creando las Autodefensas Unidas de Colombia (AUC)

descriptions: Bloque Central Bolívar was part of AUC until it became independent in 2002.
edges.evidence_quote: En el año 2000 se conformó el Bloque Central Bolívar bajo el mando militar de Javier Montañez ‘Macaco’ y de 'Julián Bolívar',y mando político de Ernesto Báez . Fusionando los grupos paramilitares de sur de Bolívar, Santander, Puerto Berrío, Yondó, Bajo Cauca y Nariño. Tras el retiro de Carlos Castaño de la jefatura principal de las AUC, se independizan de la organización en junio de 2002

descriptions: Bloque Metro is listed as an enemy of AUC in the infobox.
edges.evidence_quote: Enemigos: FARC-EP , ELN , Disidencias del EPL , ACC , Bloque Metro (Disidencia de AUC)

descriptions: Cartel del Norte del Valle is listed as an ally of AUC in the infobox.
edges.evidence_quote: Aliados: Miembros del Gobierno y de las FF.MM , Cartel del Norte del Valle

descriptions: Los Urabeños is listed as a dominant paramilitary group alongside AUC successor groups.
edges.evidence_quote: In the early 2010s, the Black Eagles, Los Rastrojos, Los Urabeños, Los Paisas, Los Machos, Renacer, Los Gaitanistas, Nueva Generación, Bloque Meta, Libertadores del Vichada, the ERPAC, and The Office of Envigado comprised the dominant criminal and paramilitary organizations.

descriptions: Autodefensas Gaitanistas de Colombia formed from former AUC members.
edges.evidence_quote: Otros integrantes de las AUC incurrieron nuevamente en su accionar delictivo, y conformaron varios grupos neoparamilitares denominados Bandas criminales emergentes (Bacrim) o Grupos Armados Organizados, entre ellos las Águilas Negras y las Autodefensas Gaitanistas de Colombia (Clan del Golfo).

descriptions: Autodefensas Gaitanistas de Colombia is a successor neoparamilitary group formed by former AUC members.
edges.evidence_quote: Otros integrantes de las AUC incurrieron nuevamente en su accionar delictivo, y conformaron varios grupos neoparamilitares denominados Bandas criminales emergentes (Bacrim) o Grupos Armados Organizados, entre ellos las Águilas Negras y las Autodefensas Gaitanistas de Colombia (Clan del Golfo).

descriptions: Autodefensas Gaitanistas de Colombia is a successor group formed by former AUC members.
edges.evidence_quote: Otros integrantes de las AUC incurrieron nuevamente en su accionar delictivo, y conformaron varios grupos neoparamilitares denominados Bandas criminales emergentes (Bacrim) o Grupos Armados Organizados, entre ellos las Águilas Negras y las Autodefensas Gaitanistas de Colombia (Clan del Golfo).

descriptions: Clan del Golfo originated from former AUC members who rearmed.
edges.evidence_quote: El primero, miembro de un grupo de criminales que anteriormente estaba al servicio del ex paramilitar Daniel Rendón Herrera , conocido por Don Mario , que inició las actividades de lo que se conoció después por la opinión pública colombiana como narcoparamilitarismo.

edges.target: Ejército Revolucionario Popular Antisubversivo de Colombia
descriptions: ERPAC is listed as a dominant paramilitary group alongside AUC successor groups.
edges.evidence_quote: In the early 2010s, the Black Eagles, Los Rastrojos, Los Urabeños, Los Paisas, Los Machos, Renacer, Los Gaitanistas, Nueva Generación, Bloque Meta, Libertadores del Vichada, the ERPAC, and The Office of Envigado comprised the dominant criminal and paramilitary organizations.

edges.target: Ejército Revolucionario Popular Antisubversivo de Colombia
descriptions: ERPAC was formed by former AUC members who did not demobilize.
edges.evidence_quote: Pedro Oliviero Guerrero Castillo , alias Cuchillo , exjefe del Ejército Revolucionario Popular Antisubversivo de Colombia (ERPAC)

descriptions: Los Paisas is listed as a dominant paramilitary group alongside AUC successor groups.
edges.evidence_quote: In the early 2010s, the Black Eagles, Los Rastrojos, Los Urabeños, Los Paisas, Los Machos, Renacer, Los Gaitanistas, Nueva Generación, Bloque Meta, Libertadores del Vichada, the ERPAC, and The Office of Envigado comprised the dominant criminal and paramilitary organizations.

descriptions: Los Paisas was founded by a former AUC member.
edges.evidence_quote: Luis Fernando era señalado por múltiples delitos, entre ellos, en la participación y posterior fundación de la bacrim Los Paisas , en complicidad con otros individuos.

descriptions: Los Rastrojos is listed as a dominant paramilitary group alongside AUC successor groups.
edges.evidence_quote: In the early 2010s, the Black Eagles, Los Rastrojos, Los Urabeños, Los Paisas, Los Machos, Renacer, Los Gaitanistas, Nueva Generación, Bloque Meta, Libertadores del Vichada, the ERPAC, and The Office of Envigado comprised the dominant criminal and paramilitary organizations.

descriptions: AUC was formed to combat ELN.
edges.evidence_quote: Se consolidó como agrupación de varios grupos paramilitares el 18 de abril de 1997 para combatir a las guerrillas como: FARC-EP , ELN y disidencias del EPL .

descriptions: The AUC was formed to combat the ELN guerrilla group.
edges.evidence_quote: Se consolidó como agrupación de varios grupos paramilitares el 18 de abril de 1997 para combatir a las guerrillas como: FARC-EP , ELN y disidencias del EPL .

descriptions: AUC considered ELN as main enemy and engaged in conflict.
edges.evidence_quote: The AUC's main enemies were the leftist insurgency groups Revolutionary Armed Forces of Colombia (FARC) and the National Liberation Army (ELN).

descriptions: The AUC conducted retaliations and attacks against the ELN.
edges.evidence_quote: The AUC was responsible for retaliations against the FARC and ELN communist organization as well as numerous attacks against civilians beginning in 1997 with the Mapiripán massacre.

descriptions: The Office of Envigado is listed as a dominant paramilitary group alongside AUC successor groups.
edges.evidence_quote: In the early 2010s, the Black Eagles, Los Rastrojos, Los Urabeños, Los Paisas, Los Machos, Renacer, Los Gaitanistas, Nueva Generación, Bloque Meta, Libertadores del Vichada, the ERPAC, and The Office of Envigado comprised the dominant criminal and paramilitary organizations.

edges.target: Peasant Self-Defenders of Córdoba and Urabá
descriptions: ACCU merged to form AUC.
edges.evidence_quote: Al conformarse las AUC el 18 de abril de 1997, las Autodefensas Campesinas de Córdoba y Urabá, las del Magdalena Medio y las de los Llanos Orientales se unieron creando las Autodefensas Unidas de Colombia (AUC)

edges.target: Peasant Self-Defenders of Córdoba and Urabá
descriptions: ACCU orchestrated the merger that formed AUC.
edges.evidence_quote: In April 1997 the AUC was formed through a merger, orchestrated by the ACCU, of local right-wing militias.

edges.target: Peasant Self-Defenders of Córdoba and Urabá
descriptions: The ACCU orchestrated the merger that formed the AUC.
edges.evidence_quote: In April 1997 the AUC was formed through a merger, orchestrated by the ACCU, of local right-wing militias.

descriptions: AUC was formed to combat disidencias of EPL.
edges.evidence_quote: Se consolidó como agrupación de varios grupos paramilitares el 18 de abril de 1997 para combatir a las guerrillas como: FARC-EP , ELN y disidencias del EPL .

descriptions: The AUC was formed to combat dissidents of the EPL guerrilla group.
edges.evidence_quote: Se consolidó como agrupación de varios grupos paramilitares el 18 de abril de 1997 para combatir a las guerrillas como: FARC-EP , ELN y disidencias del EPL .

descriptions: The AUC was formed to combat dissidences of the EPL guerrilla group.
edges.evidence_quote: Se consolidó como agrupación de varios grupos paramilitares el 18 de abril de 1997 para combatir a las guerrillas como: FARC-EP , ELN y disidencias del EPL .

descriptions: AUC fought against FARC-EP as part of counterinsurgency.
edges.evidence_quote: As a response, the AUC engaged in a renewed series of massacres and assassinations, often with the passive or active aid of elements of the Colombian government's security forces, according to human rights organizations.

descriptions: AUC fought against FARC.
edges.evidence_quote: the United Self-Defense Forces of Colombia (AUC), a paramilitary death squad that was formed not only for the purpose of fighting the Cartel but also the Revolutionary Armed Forces of Colombia (FARC)

descriptions: AUC considered FARC as main enemy and engaged in conflict.
edges.evidence_quote: The AUC's main enemies were the leftist insurgency groups Revolutionary Armed Forces of Colombia (FARC) and the National Liberation Army (ELN).

descriptions: The AUC conducted retaliations and attacks against the FARC.
edges.evidence_quote: The AUC was responsible for retaliations against the FARC and ELN communist organization as well as numerous attacks against civilians beginning in 1997 with the Mapiripán massacre.

descriptions: Black Eagles is listed as a dominant paramilitary group alongside AUC successor groups.
edges.evidence_quote: In the early 2010s, the Black Eagles, Los Rastrojos, Los Urabeños, Los Paisas, Los Machos, Renacer, Los Gaitanistas, Nueva Generación, Bloque Meta, Libertadores del Vichada, the ERPAC, and The Office of Envigado comprised the dominant criminal and paramilitary organizations.

descriptions: Águilas Negras formed from former AUC members.
edges.evidence_quote: Otros integrantes de las AUC incurrieron nuevamente en su accionar delictivo, y conformaron varios grupos neoparamilitares denominados Bandas criminales emergentes (Bacrim) o Grupos Armados Organizados, entre ellos las Águilas Negras y las Autodefensas Gaitanistas de Colombia (Clan del Golfo).

descriptions: Águilas Negras is a successor neoparamilitary group formed by former AUC members.
edges.evidence_quote: Otros integrantes de las AUC incurrieron nuevamente en su accionar delictivo, y conformaron varios grupos neoparamilitares denominados Bandas criminales emergentes (Bacrim) o Grupos Armados Organizados, entre ellos las Águilas Negras y las Autodefensas Gaitanistas de Colombia (Clan del Golfo).

descriptions: Águilas Negras is a successor group formed by former AUC members.
edges.evidence_quote: Otros integrantes de las AUC incurrieron nuevamente en su accionar delictivo, y conformaron varios grupos neoparamilitares denominados Bandas criminales emergentes (Bacrim) o Grupos Armados Organizados, entre ellos las Águilas Negras y las Autodefensas Gaitanistas de Colombia (Clan del Golfo).

descriptions: Black Eagles are a successor group to AUC.
edges.evidence_quote: However, local successors such as the Black Eagles continue to exist and death threats have been made using its name.

descriptions: AUC is listed as an enemy of Bloque Metro from 2002 in the infobox.
edges.evidence_quote: Enemigos: Autodefensas Unidas de Colombia (Desde 2002)

descriptions: Bloque Metro was later linked to AUC.
edges.evidence_quote: Estaba vinculado a las Autodefensas Campesinas de Córdoba y Urabá (ACCU) y posteriormente a las Autodefensas Unidas de Colombia (AUC)

descriptions: Bloque Metro was later linked to AUC as part of its structure.
edges.evidence_quote: Estaba vinculado a las Autodefensas Campesinas de Córdoba y Urabá (ACCU) y posteriormente a las Autodefensas Unidas de Colombia (AUC)

descriptions: Bloque Norte de las AUC took over the Costa cartel's drug routes after its dissolution.
edges.evidence_quote: El Bloque Norte de las AUC se apropió de las rutas del narcotráfico del cartel de la Costa

descriptions: North Coast Cartel hired AUC for protection of drug routes and labs.
edges.evidence_quote: The cartel is believed to have employed the services of the United Self-Defense Forces of Colombia (AUC), a right-wing paramilitary organization internationally classified as a terrorist organization, to protect the cartel's drug routes, its drug laboratories, and its members and associates.

descriptions: Cartel de La Guajira supplied weapons to the Bloque Norte de las AUC.
edges.evidence_quote: El cartel también trafica con armas en el mercado negro, siendo proveedor del extinto del grupo narcoparamilitar Bloque Norte de las Autodefensas Unidas de Colombia (AUC).

descriptions: The collapse of the Medellín Cartel allowed paramilitary groups like the AUC to take over the cocaine trade.
edges.evidence_quote: The downfall of the Medellín and Cali Cartels in the 1990s created an opening for paramilitary groups, which controlled northern Colombia (the key transnational smuggling route), to take over the international cocaine trade.

descriptions: The AUC descended directly from the Cartel de Medellín.
edges.evidence_quote: Otra organización, de tendencia paramilitar, llamada las Autodefensas Unidas de Colombia (AUC) descendía de forma directa del Cartel de Medellín

descriptions: The Cartel del Norte del Valle hired the AUC to protect its drug routes and labs.
edges.evidence_quote: los miembros del cartel emplearon los servicios de las Autodefensas Unidas de Colombia (AUC), una organización paramilitar de derecha internacionalmente clasificada como terrorista, para proteger las rutas de la droga del cartel

descriptions: The Norte del Valle Cartel employed the AUC for protection of drug routes and labs.
edges.evidence_quote: The indictment alleges that the cartel members employed the services of the United Self-Defense Forces of Colombia (AUC), a right-wing paramilitary organization internationally classified as terrorist, to protect the cartel's drug routes, its drug laboratories, and its members and associates.

descriptions: Clan del Golfo emerged after the demobilization of AUC.
edges.evidence_quote: surgió tras la desmovilización entre 2003 y 2006 de las Autodefensas Unidas de Colombia o (AUC).

descriptions: Clan del Golfo emerged after the demobilization of the AUC.
edges.evidence_quote: surgió tras la desmovilización entre 2003 y 2006 de las Autodefensas Unidas de Colombia o (AUC)

descriptions: AGC emerged after AUC demobilization, indicating a successor relationship.
edges.evidence_quote: The AGC is one of the organizations that appeared after the demobilization of the United Self-Defense Forces of Colombia (AUC).

edges.source: Ejército Revolucionario Popular Antisubversivo de Colombia
descriptions: ERPAC inherited military and drug trafficking spaces from AUC's Bloque Centauros.
edges.evidence_quote: Era el heredero de los espacios militares y de narcotráfico del Bloque Centauros de las Autodefensas Unidas de Colombia

descriptions: Los Caparrapos were part of the AUC.
edges.evidence_quote: Este grupo y otros más harían parte de las Autodefensas Unidas de Colombia (AUC) de Vicente Castaño.

descriptions: Miembros de Los Pepes se convirtieron en líderes de las AUC.
edges.evidence_quote: Otros se consolidarían como líderes de las autodefensas, que se agruparían años después bajo el nombre de AUC

descriptions: Former Los Pepes leaders became leaders of AUC.
edges.evidence_quote: several of their leaders eventually went on to become leaders of a national paramilitary alliance in Colombia, the United Self-Defense Forces of Colombia (AUC)

descriptions: Los Rastrojos members conspired with AUC commander Macaco to murder Wilber Varela.
edges.evidence_quote: Authorities now believe Wilber Varela's main captains Diego Perez Henao alias 'Diego Rastrojo' and Luis Enrique Calle alias 'Combatiente' ('Combatant') conspired with AUC commander Carlos Mario Jiménez alias 'Macaco' to murder Varela.

descriptions: Los Rastrojos were formed by dissidents of the AUC.
edges.evidence_quote: Fue compuesta por disidentes de las Autodefensas Unidas de Colombia (AUC)

descriptions: Los Rastrojos was formed by dissidents of the AUC.
edges.evidence_quote: Fue compuesta por disidentes de las Autodefensas Unidas de Colombia (AUC)

descriptions: AUC paramilitaries conducted anti-guerrilla operations against ELN, indicating conflict.
edges.evidence_quote: A Colombian government initiative towards granting the ELN a demilitarized zone in the southern region of the Bolívar Department was thwarted by right-wing political pressure from the United Self-Defense Forces of Colombia (AUC) whose paramilitary mercenaries conduct anti-guerrilla operations in that part of the department.

descriptions: La Oficina de Envigado affiliated with the United Self-Defense Forces of Colombia to organize drug trafficking.
edges.evidence_quote: The organization then inherited the Medellín turf and its criminal connections in the US, Mexico, and the UK, and began to affiliate with the paramilitary United Self-Defense Forces of Colombia, organizing drug trafficking operations on their behalf.

descriptions: Oficina de Envigado affiliated with AUC, organizing drug trafficking for them.
edges.evidence_quote: Murillo then began to affiliate his Oficina with the United Self-Defense Forces of Colombia

descriptions: Oficina de Envigado was initially part of the AUC.
edges.evidence_quote: La Oficina de Envigado, que empezó siendo parte de las Autodefensas Unidas de Colombia (AUC).

descriptions: Oficina de Envigado became part of the AUC as the Bloque Cacique Nutibara.
edges.evidence_quote: La Oficina comenzó a funcionar pero ahora como parte de las Autodefensas Unidas de Colombia (AUC) llamándose Bloque Cacique Nutibara

edges.source: Peasant Self-Defenders of Córdoba and Urabá
descriptions: ACCU led to the formation of AUC and became its most powerful component.
edges.evidence_quote: After Fidel Castaño's disappearance, his brother Carlos took over the ACCU and eventually led it to the formation of the United Self-Defenders of Colombia (AUC), which grouped other small paramilitary armies organized throughout Colombia.

descriptions: EPL considered AUC as enemies until 2006.
edges.evidence_quote: Enemigos: AUC ( Hasta 2006 )

descriptions: AUC leaders expressed support for anti-FARC protests, indicating opposition to FARC.
edges.evidence_quote: Shortly before the rallies took place 13 demobilised AUC paramilitary leaders, including Salvatore Mancuso, had expressed their support of the protest through a communique. However, this move was rejected by organiser Carlos Andrés Santiago, who stated that such an endorsement was harmful and criticised the AUC's actions.

edges.source: Servicios Especiales De Vigilancia y Seguridad Privada
descriptions: CONVIVIR groups merged into the AUC.
edges.evidence_quote: Many of the CONVIVIR groups simply joined up with the Autodefensas Unidas de Colombia (AUC).

descriptions: Cártel de Sinaloa established contacts with AUC paramilitaries to traffic Colombian cocaine.
edges.evidence_quote: apoderándose del tráfico de cocaína colombiana en los mercados mundiales gracias a los contactos establecidos con la guerrilla de las FARC y los paramilitares de las AUC

descriptions: Some members of Águilas Negras were part of the demobilization of AUC, indicating a structural link.
edges.evidence_quote: Algunos de sus miembros formaron parte de la desmovilización de las Autodefensas Unidas de Colombia (AUC).

descriptions: Black Eagles formed from preexisting AUC forces after demobilization.
edges.evidence_quote: Black Eagles ... emerged from the failures of the demobilization process between 2004 and 2006, which aimed to disarm the United Self-Defense Units of Colombia (AUC).

descriptions: Tehreek-e-Khilafat (Pakistan) is an ally of ISIS–K.
edges.evidence_quote: Allies: ... Tehreek-e-Khilafat (Pakistan)

descriptions: Infobox lists Rural Defense Force as an ally.
edges.evidence_quote: Allies: Rural Defense Force

descriptions: The Romito clan (through its successor) is in constant conflict with the Li Bergolis-Miucci clan.
edges.evidence_quote: Pasquale Ricucci, 45 anni, ritenuto dagli investigatori al vertice del clan Lombardi-Ricucci-La Torre, erede dei Romito, da sempre in lotta con il clan dei 'montanari' Li Bergolis-Miucci

descriptions: The Lombardi-Ricucci-La Torre clan is in constant conflict with the Li Bergolis-Miucci clan.
edges.evidence_quote: Pasquale Ricucci, 45 anni, ritenuto dagli investigatori al vertice del clan Lombardi-Ricucci-La Torre, erede dei Romito, da sempre in lotta con il clan dei 'montanari' Li Bergolis-Miucci

descriptions: Unit 180 is affiliated with Lazarus Group.
edges.evidence_quote: Affiliations: Lab 110, Bureau 121, Unit 180, AndAriel

descriptions: ISIS–K formed an alliance with Harakat Islami Tajikistan.
edges.evidence_quote: the newly ordained ISIS–K forged significant alliances with other Sunni jihadist movements in the region to include the Islamic Movement of Uzbekistan (IMU), Islamic Movement of Turkmenistan (IMT), Uyghur East Turkestan Islamic Party (ETIP), Jamaat Ansarullah, Harakat Islami Tajikistan, and the Islamic Jihad Renaissance Party of Tajikistan (IJRPT), Hezb-e Islami Gulbuddin, and Balochi militant groups including the Pakistani Jundullah movement and Iranian Jundullah movement

descriptions: The Caia-Laganà-Gioffrè faction split into Caia-Gioffrè and Laganà, which then conflicted.
edges.evidence_quote: successivamente sono entrati in conflitto scindendosi in Caia-Gioffrè e Laganà

descriptions: The Caia-Laganà-Gioffrè faction split into Caia-Gioffrè and Laganà, which then conflicted.
edges.evidence_quote: successivamente sono entrati in conflitto scindendosi in Caia-Gioffrè e Laganà

descriptions: Internal feud between the Gioffrè faction ('ndoli) and the Caia-Laganà-Gioffrè faction ('ngrisi).
edges.evidence_quote: faida interna tra i Gioffrè di Seminara ( 'ndoli ) e i Caia-Laganà-Gioffrè detti 'ngrisi

descriptions: Brother Speed allegedly associated with Outsiders to acquire methamphetamine precursor chemicals.
edges.evidence_quote: The Brother Speed has allegedly associated with the Hells Angels, the Outsiders, and the Devils Breed in order to acquire precursor chemicals used in the manufacture of methamphetamine.

No Limit Soldiers.description: Organized crime group and street gang from Curaçao, originating in the impoverished neighborhood of Koraal Specht, Willemstad. Involved in high-intensity drug trafficking into the Netherlands and contract killings. Connected to the murder of politician Helmin Wiels. Operates cells in Dutch cities like Rotterdam, The Hague, and Amsterdam.

descriptions: No Limit Soldiers member acted as representative for 'Ndrangheta in Brazil.
edges.evidence_quote: a criminal network that included Chendal Wilfrido Rosa, a member of the No Limit Soldiers who was acting as a representative for 'Ndrangheta in Brazil

descriptions: Zoe Pound is listed as an ally of No Limit Soldiers in the infobox.
edges.evidence_quote: Allies: Colombian cartels , Penose , Yardies , Zoe Pound

descriptions: Clan Licciardi is in internal conflict with Sacco and Bocchetti over autonomy.
edges.evidence_quote: attualmente vive un conflitto interno a causa delle rivendicazioni di autonomia delle famiglie Sacco e Bocchetti di S. Pietro a Patierno.

descriptions: Members of Bones joined Hells Angels in taking over Hamburg Kiez.
edges.evidence_quote: The other Hells Angels members and 250 of 497 members of the motorcycle club 'Bones' in Hannover under its President Frank Hanebuth, took over the power in the Hamburg Kiez

descriptions: The Sangermano family was a satellite group of Clan Cava, later split into an independent organization.
edges.evidence_quote: Nel Nolano, nella fattispecie, i Cava hanno sempre operato mediante un gruppo satellite facente capo alla famiglia Sangermano, originaria di San Paolo Bel Sito , oggi considerata scissa dal clan Cava ed una organizzazione a sé stante.

Gustin Gang.description: The Gustin Gang was an Irish-American gang active in Boston during the Prohibition era, formed in the mid-1910s by Frank Wallace and his brother Steve. They engaged in hijackings, armed robbery, and bootlegging, using false badges to confiscate alcohol from rivals. The gang dominated Boston's underworld in the 1920s but declined after Frank Wallace's death in 1931.

descriptions: Gustin Gang listed as rival in infobox.
edges.evidence_quote: Rivals: Charlestown Gang, Gustin Gang, Winter Hill Gang, and various other gangs in New England

descriptions: Fazzari members are in close contact with the Di Silvio clan in Rome.
edges.evidence_quote: esponenti della Famiglia Fazzari in stretto contatto con le locali organizzazioni criminali, tra cui ... i Di Silvio

descriptions: Members of the two clans clashed in a violent incident in 1969.
edges.evidence_quote: Il 23 gennaio 1969 al Mandrione scoppiano incidenti fra un centinaio di persone, che vedono fronteggiarsi membri del clan Casamonica e membri del clan Di Silvio.

descriptions: The Casamonica, Spada, Di Silvio, and De Rosa families jointly operated a surveillance system for their criminal activities.
edges.evidence_quote: Nel gennaio del 2012, ... i Casamonica, ma anche gli Spada, i Di Silvio, i De Rosa, avevano installato all'interno delle abitazioni un sistema di video sorveglianza ...

descriptions: Di Silvio family is affiliated with Casamonica clan.
edges.evidence_quote: Police operations that led to the arrest not only of members of the Clan, but also of members of other affiliated families of Sinti origin, such as the Spada and the Di Silvio

descriptions: Booze Runners bombed Hell's Henchmen clubhouse as part of Outlaws' conflict.
edges.evidence_quote: On November 13, 1990, the Booze Runners, a gang founded and led by Kevin "Spike" O'Neill which was vying for chapter status in the Outlaws, bombed the Hell's Henchmen clubhouse in Rockford

descriptions: Hell's Henchmen socialized with Hells Angels and later agreed to prospect for them.
edges.evidence_quote: members of the Hell's Henchmen partied with Hells Angels at bars in Chicago, Calumet City and Alsip.

descriptions: Hells Angels absorbed Hell's Henchmen to expand into Chicago.
edges.evidence_quote: The Hells Angels decided to absorb the Hell's Henchmen in order to gain a foothold in the large and lucrative drug trade in the Chicago area

descriptions: Outlaws attempted to coerce Hell's Henchmen to join them, leading to conflict.
edges.evidence_quote: The Outlaws attempted to coerce the Hell's Henchmen, one of the oldest and largest motorcycle gangs in the Chicago area, into "patching over" to join the Outlaws

descriptions: Outlaws carried out bombings and murder attempts against Hell's Henchmen members.
edges.evidence_quote: The Outlaws then tried to kill Roger Fiebrantz, Mathias' successor as Rockford Hell's Henchmen president.
